Run the deep manual-submit remote job search for Klajdi using the current rules and saved applicant profile. Search broadly across the web for strong-fit AI/devtools/software roles in Europe, US, and Australia only. Exclude Albania, Balkans-only roles, relocation-required roles, onsite-only roles, scam/commission-only roles. Optimize for acceptance probability: explicit remote eligibility, stack/seniority match, recency, lower blocker count, and realistic attainability. Use specialist subagents if helpful. Output should be suitable for Discord #briefing and list the best manual-submit roles with: company, role, canonical URL, remote/location note, why it fits, why likely attainable, and blocker/risk. If no worthwhile additions are found, say so explicitly.

Continue Klajdi's remote job search until told otherwise. Search broadly across the web for strong-fit AI/devtools/software roles in Europe, US, and Australia only. Exclude Albania, Balkans-only roles, relocation-required roles, onsite-only roles, scam/commission-only roles. Use Klajdi's saved applicant profile: Tirana, Albania; both contract and full-time; US visa sponsorship required; earliest start date 2026-04-02; GitHub https://github.com/klajdikkolaj ; LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/in/klajdi-kolaj-a1864377/ ; CV available in local workspace/media. Do not focus only on Anthropic/OpenAI; include startups, scaleups, devtools, automation, AI product companies, and high-fit ATS listings from across the web. At the end of each run, send a concise Discord #briefing report listing the applications Klajdi should manually submit now. For each recommended role include: company, role, canonical URL, remote/location eligibility note, why it fits, and any blocker/risk (for example: ATS spam block, ambiguous location, browser/form issue). If there are no worthwhile additions, say that explicitly and summarize why.

payload.message:
Run weekly procurement research focused on software development opportunities.

Scope (required):
- projects.worldbank.org (procurement / consulting / IT/software related)
- AKSHI opportunities/tenders
- Other Albanian public institutions publishing procurement notices

Filtering rules:
1) Include only opportunities relevant to software development, software implementation, digital platforms, systems integration, custom development, maintenance/support, data platforms, or IT services that require a software company.
2) Include only items where bidding/application expiration date is at least 30 days after today (research date).
3) Exclude expired, irrelevant hardware-only procurement, and non-software categories.

For each selected opportunity, provide:
- Title
- Institution / buyer
- Country/region
- Publication date (if available)
- Expiration/bid deadline
- Why it fits software-development companies (2-4 bullet brief)
- Link

Output requirements:
- Create Markdown report at: /home/clawdkbot/virgil-vault/Opportunities/Public-Procurement/YYYY-MM-DD-procurement-report.md
- Include frontmatter: date, sources_checked, total_found, total_qualified, min_deadline_rule: "+30d"
- Include a short executive summary at top.

Git sync (mandatory):
1) cd /home/clawdkbot/virgil-vault
2) git pull --rebase
3) git add Opportunities/Public-Procurement/*.md
4) if staged changes exist:
   - git commit -m "chore(procurement): weekly software opportunities $(date +%F)"
   - git push origin HEAD
5) if no changes, report "no changes to sync"

Completion message (concise):
- qualified count
- saved file path
- git sync result
- top 3 highest-value links
